name: brenthy-tools-beta

metadata.name: brenthy-tools-beta
metadata.summary: A library for interacting with Brenthy (`brenthy_api`) and a collection of tools common to Brenthy Core and `brenthy_api`.

documentation:
# Brenthy
Here lives the source code for Brenthy, the framework for developing and deploying new kinds of blockchain in the Python programming language.

## Learn about Brenthy:

Main docs:
- [Introduction to Brenthy](/Documentation/Brenthy/Meaning/IntroductionToBrenthy.md)
- [Running from Source](/Documentation/Brenthy/User/RunningFromSource.md)
- [Installation](/Documentation/Brenthy/User/InstallingBrenthy.md)
- Create Blockchains for Brenthy: _coming soon..._

Overview of all docs: [/Documentation/DocsOverview.md](/Documentation/DocsOverview.md)


## Walytis
Walytis is the novel blockchain Brenthy was originally built for.
Read the [Introduction to Walytis](/Documentation/Walytis/Meaning/IntroductionToWalytis.md) to learn more.
The source code for Walytis is located under [./blockchains/Walytis_Beta](./blockchains/Walytis_Beta/ReadMe.md)

modules.name: brenthy_tools_beta.brenthy_api_protocols.bap_3_brenthy_tools
documentation:
Brenthy API Protocol version 1, on Brenthy-Tool's side.

This module contains the machinery used by brenthy_tools.brenthy_api for
BrenthyAPI communication with Brenthy Core, using the version 1 BrenthyAPI
Protocol.
This module's counterpart, which contain's Brenthy Core's machinery, is at
../../api_terminal/brenthy_api_protocols/bap_3_brenthy_core.py

functions.name: brenthy_tools_beta.brenthy_api_protocols.bap_3_brenthy_tools.send_request
documentation:
Send a BrenthyAPI request to Brenthy-Core, returning its reply.

Args:
    request (bytearray): the data to send to Brenthy-Core
Returns:
    bytearray: the response received from Brenthy-Core

methods.name: brenthy_tools_beta.brenthy_api_protocols.bap_4_brenthy_tools.EventListener.__init__
documentation:
Listen for events from Brenthy Core.

Args:
    eventhandler (FuncType): a function that takes as input a
        dict (event-data) and optionally a string (topic)
        See class docstring for examples.
    topics (list[str] | str): the topics to filter messages by

methods.name: brenthy_tools_beta.brenthy_api_protocols.bap_4_brenthy_tools.EventListener._listen
documentation: Listen for messages and call user's eventhandler when received.

methods.name: brenthy_tools_beta.brenthy_api_protocols.bap_4_brenthy_tools.EventListener.terminate
documentation: Stop listening for events and clean up resources.

methods.name: brenthy_tools_beta.brenthy_api_protocols.bap_4_brenthy_tools.EventListener.__del__
documentation: Stop listening for events and clean up resources.

Object for listening to events published by Brenthy Core.

Asks the Brenthy API Terminal to notify us when a certain blockchain type
publishes events, optionally only events from the specified set of topics,
calling the provided eventhandler function when such a topic is received.

Examples of eventhandlers:
```python
def _on_new_block_received(data: dict):
   pass

def _on_new_block_received(data: dict, topic:str):
   pass
```

modules.name: brenthy_tools_beta
documentation:
The library for interacting with Brenthy.

This package contains the library `brenthy_api` for interacting with
Brenthy Core, as well as utilities common to both Brenthy Core and
`brenthy_api`.

modules.name: brenthy_tools_beta.version_utils
documentation: Utilities for processing blockchain version codes.

functions.name: brenthy_tools_beta.version_utils.encode_version
documentation:
Encode tuple version identifier into a bytearray with no [0] elements.

Args:
    version (list): a list of integers that designate a blockchain version
Returns:
    bytearray: the encoded version identifier, with no [0]s
pylint:disable=unused-variable

functions.name: brenthy_tools_beta.version_utils.decode_version
documentation:
Decode bytearray version identifiers encoded by encode_version().

Parameter:
    bytearray data: the the encoded version identifier
Returns:
list version: the decoded version identifier (as an array of integers)

functions.name: brenthy_tools_beta.version_utils.is_version_greater
documentation:
Check whether or not the first is a higher version than the second.

Returns:
    bool result: whether or not the first version is higher than the second

functions.name: brenthy_tools_beta.version_utils.version_from_string
documentation: Convert a version string to a tuple.

functions.name: brenthy_tools_beta.version_utils.version_to_string
documentation: Convert a version tuple to a string.

functions.name: brenthy_tools_beta.utils.to_b255_no_0s
documentation:
Encode an integer into bytearray that doesn't have any [0]s.

E.g 0 is encoded to [1], 1 is encoded to [2], 255 is encoded to [2,1]

Args:
    number(int): the integer value to encode
Returns:
    bytearray: the input number encoded as a bytearray without 0s or 1s

functions.name: brenthy_tools_beta.utils.from_b255_no_0s
documentation:
Decode data encoded with to_b255_no_0s back to the original integer.

Args:
    array (bytearray): the encoded data to decode
Returns:
    int: the decoded value

functions.name: brenthy_tools_beta.utils.to_b254_no_0s1s
documentation:
Encode the input integer into a bytearray without any [0] or [1]s.

E.g 0 is encoded to [2], 1 is encoded to [3], 254 is encoded to [3,2]

Args:
    number (int): the integer value to encode
Returns:
    bytearray: the input number encoded as a bytearray without 0s or 1s

functions.name: brenthy_tools_beta.utils.from_b254_no_0s1s
documentation:
Decode an integer encoded with to_b254_no_0s1s.

Args:
    data (bytearray): the encoded data to decode
Returns:
    int: the decoded value

functions.name: brenthy_tools_beta.utils.bytes_to_b255_no_0s
documentation: Encode a bytearray into a bytearray without [0]s.

functions.name: brenthy_tools_beta.utils.bytes_from_b255_no_0s
documentation: Decode a byetarray encoded with bytes_to_b255_no_0s.

documentation: Print a bytearray as a list of each integer.

functions.name: brenthy_tools_beta.utils.printable_bytearray
documentation: Convert a bytearray into a string listing each integer in the array.

functions.name: brenthy_tools_beta.utils.bytes_to_string

documentation: The network addresses via which Brenthy uses for BrenthyAPI.

modules.name: brenthy_tools_beta.bt_endpoints
documentation:
The websocket-level communication machinery for BrenthyAPI.

This file contains all the websocket-level communication machinery
`brenthy_api` needs to communicate with Brenthy via all BrenthyAPI protocols.
More specifically, it contains different functions and classes for publishing
events and receiving & replying to requests via ZMQ & TCP sockets
This file has a counterpart under ../brenthy_tools_beta/bt_endpoints.py.
'bt' in bt_endpoints tands for Brenthy Tools, to distinguish this
file's name from bt_endpoints.py, where 'bat' stands for Brenthy API Terminal.

functions.name: brenthy_tools_beta.bt_endpoints.initialise
documentation: Reinitialising them if they have been cleaned up.

functions.name: brenthy_tools_beta.bt_endpoints.send_request_zmq
documentation:
Send a request to the given address, expecting a reply.

Args:
    request (bytearray): the data to send
    socket_address (tuple[str,int]): IP address and port number to send to
    timeout (int): how long to wait before giving up
Returns:
    bytearray: reply received from the endpoint after sending the request

functions.name: brenthy_tools_beta.bt_endpoints.send_request_tcp
documentation:
Send a request to the given address, expecting a reply.

Args:
    request (bytearray): the data to send
    socket_address (tuple[str,int]): IP address and port number to send to
Returns:
    bytearray: reply received from the endpoint after sending the request

modules.name: brenthy_tools_beta.brenthy_api
documentation: Library for interacting with Brenthy Core and its blockchain types.

functions.name: brenthy_tools_beta.brenthy_api.send_request
documentation:
Send a request to Brenthy or one of its installed blockchain types.

Args:
    blockchain_type(str): the blockchain type to forward the payload to
        use 'Brenthy' if the request is to Brenthy itself
    payload(bytearray): the message to send to Brenthy or the blockchain
Returns:
    bytearray: the reply from Brenthy or the blockchain.

functions.name: brenthy_tools_beta.brenthy_api._analyse_no_success_reply
documentation:
Get the appropriate Exception for the given reply from Brenthy.

Assumes the reply is from a request which Brenthy (not its blockchains)
failed to execute.

Args:
    reply (bytearray): the response from Brenthy for the failed RPC

functions.name: brenthy_tools_beta.brenthy_api.send_brenthy_request
documentation:
Make a request to Brenthy (NOT a Brenthy blockchain).

Args:
    function_name (str): the name of the function in api_terminal
                            which we want to call
    payload (bytearray): the data the function in api_terminal
                            needs to process our request, its arguments
Returns:
    bytearray: the reply from the function we called in
                            api_terminal
